score_details.score_summary: The Intercept Media Inc shows good impact and limited alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://connexions.org/CxLibrary/CX16565.htm)

all_citations.claim: The Intercept Media Inc. focuses on investigative journalism and maintains specific financial efficiency metrics.
The organization reported total revenue of $5,597,043, a program expense ratio of 71.7%, 8.3 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.26 per $1 raised.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (sadaqah) to support its mission of adversarial journalism.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates solid financial management and operational efficiency, though its specific focus on advocacy and secular journalism results in a moderate alignment for faith-based donors.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment with traditional Muslim donor priorities and religious giving frameworks
Dependency on advocacy-based funding models which may impact long-term stability

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated as medium because the mission focuses on general civic advocacy rather than specific community-based or religious services [1]. While the organization is sadaqah-eligible, its primary focus on investigative journalism represents a low donor fit for those seeking traditional humanitarian or faith-centered outcomes [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization shows moderate impact by focusing on capacity building within the media landscape, allocating a 71.7% program expense ratio toward its investigative missions [1][2]. With 8.3 months of working capital, the charity maintains a stable financial foundation to continue its documentary and reporting programs without immediate fiscal distress [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.
strengths: Maintains a program expense ratio of 71.7%, ensuring the majority of funds support journalism
Strong liquidity with 8.3 months of working capital available
Efficient fundraising operations spending only $0.26 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. is a non-profit news organization dedicated to holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable through adversarial journalism [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $5,597,043 to support its core programs in investigative reporting, podcasting, and documentary filmmaking [2].

all_citations.quote: At The Intercept, we investigate powerful individuals and institutions to expose corruption and injustice.
Geographic Coverage: USA, Syria, Iran, Mexico, Ecuador
Top Domestic Recipients: DROP SITE NEWS INC: $280,000

all_citations.claim: Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates solid financial management and operational efficiency, though its specific focus on advocacy and secular journalism results in a moderate alignment for faith-based donors.

areas_for_improvement.context: The current board consists of only <cite id="12">4 members</cite>, which is smaller than the recommended size for an organization of this scale.
While the organization tracks <cite id="13">one primary outcome metric</cite> via Candid, publishing more granular beneficiary data would improve cost-effectiveness assessments.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB assessment data is currently unavailable for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization mitigates governance risks through high public transparency and a Platinum Candid rating.
risk_factors: <cite id="12">Small board size (4 members)</cite> limits diverse oversight
case_against.summary: The primary limitations involve <cite id="12">minimal board governance</cite> and a <cite id="14">lack of religious giving infrastructure</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Data from 2023 Form 990 and 2024/2025 website/rating crawls
confidence.notes: Strong financial and transparency data available.

alignment.explanation: The charity is <cite id="14">Sadaqah-eligible</cite> and serves as a watchdog for institutional injustice, though it lacks <cite id="14">specific Muslim donor alignment signals</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="14">clarifying its relevance to faith-based social justice priorities</cite>.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and clear <cite id="15">documented theory of change</cite>. The organization is led by <cite id="16">CEO Annie Chabel</cite> and maintains <cite id="17">independent financial audits</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="12">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent oversight members.

impact.explanation: Operations are focused on capacity building for the journalism sector, with a <cite id="5">71.7% program ratio</cite> and <cite id="7">low fundraising costs</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="13">tracking outcomes over a longer period</cite> and seeking external evaluations of their reporting's policy influence.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Outcome tracking via Candid

rich_narrative.headline: The Intercept Media Inc. serves as a critical watchdog, utilizing investigative journalism to expose corruption and institutional injustice.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value investigative journalism as a tool for social justice and want to support high-transparency watchdog organizations.
donor_motivations: Believe in holding powerful institutions accountable
Prefer supporting established media entities with a global reach
Want to fund adversarial reporting on corruption
giving_considerations: The organization focuses on systemic change rather than direct aid
Impact is measured through journalistic influence rather than individual beneficiary counts
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for direct humanitarian relief like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that outcomes are measured and a theory of change is documented, but independent evaluations are missing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Intercept uses investigative journalism to inform the public and spark civic action against corruption.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in journalism is often measured by policy changes or public awareness shifts resulting from reporting.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The organization operates in a high-cost investigative field with significant unmet reporting needs.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">adversarial approach</cite> and <cite id="11">Platinum-level transparency</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Larger scale and more Pulitzer wins
similar_organizations.name: ProPublica

similar_organizations.differentiator: Heavier focus on local US reporting
similar_organizations.name: Center for Investigative Reporting

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="7">$0.26 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than the standard industry benchmark of $0.25.
With <cite id="6">8.3 months of working capital</cite> available, the charity is well-positioned to withstand funding fluctuations.
The Intercept has earned a <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The Intercept demonstrates a commitment to financial transparency and lean operations. By maintaining a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, the organization ensures that the bulk of its <cite id="3">$18.9 million revenue</cite> is utilized for investigative reporting and transparency support. Their <cite id="11">Platinum Candid Seal</cite> reflects a willingness to share comprehensive data regarding their mission and results. Furthermore, the organization has diversified its impact through its 'Field of Vision' documentary unit and podcasts, broadening the reach of its <cite id="1">watchdog journalism</cite> beyond traditional print media.
rich_narrative.summary: The Intercept Media Inc. operates as a non-profit news organization dedicated to <cite id="1">holding powerful individuals and institutions accountable</cite> through adversarial journalism. Since its <cite id="2">founding in 2014</cite>, the organization has focused on investigative reporting, podcasting, and documentary filmmaking to expose corruption and institutional injustice. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $18,961,623</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">$18.7 million in public contributions</cite>. The charity maintains a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 71.7%</cite>, directing the majority of its resources toward its editorial mission. Financial health is a notable strength, as the organization holds <cite id="6">8.3 months of working capital</cite> to ensure operational resilience. Fundraising operations are highly efficient, costing only <cite id="7">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite>. While the organization serves a global audience, its primary operations are based in the <cite id="8">United States</cite>, with reporting coverage extending to regions including <cite id="9">Syria, Iran, and Mexico</cite>. The Intercept also engages in strategic grantmaking, recently providing <cite id="10">$280,000 to Drop Site News Inc.</cite> to support independent journalism.
